title: Add an auxiliary laps-to-next-stop target and feed its OOF prediction back into the classifier
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work (SOSTA AI) uses a cascaded multi-task design where the binary next-lap call is complemented by estimating how many laps remain until the stop.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work (SOSTA AI) uses a cascaded multi-task design where the binary next-lap call is complemented by estimating how many laps remain until the stop. That auxiliary horizon should encode smoother strategy information than a pure one-step label.
implementation_hint: Create `laps_to_next_pit` on train data, with censored handling at race end. Train an out-of-fold regressor or ordinal model for this horizon, then add `pred_horizon`, `1/(pred_horizon+1)`, and coarse horizon bins as features to the main classifier.
expected_effect: Should sharpen predictions in the 1–5 lap pre-stop region where binary labels are noisy but the strategic intent is already visible.
risk: Label generation can leak if the horizon is built across fold boundaries without care; censored rows need a consistent convention.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
